core_mechanism: resolution of singularities: a birational blow-up that transforms a function with an isolated singular zero into a locally monomial normal form, so that the local integrability of a negative power of that function is governed by an explicit critical exponent (a log-canonical threshold) read off from the vanishing orders and discrepancy exponents of the resolution
brief_reason: The abstract states the admissible exponent threshold p < min_i (a_i+1)/(2N_i - M_i) is expressed through vanishing orders and discrepancy exponents from a log-resolution, with the real log-canonical threshold as a computable bound, and that the proof reduces the problem to a monomial model via resolution of singularities to obtain the integrability estimates.
